concepts.title: Current Ratio
concepts.description: Current Ratio is calculated by dividing Total Current Assets by Total Current Liabilities. It represents the business's ability to pay off its short-term debts.

concepts.title: Debt Equity Ratio
concepts.description: Debt Equity Ratio is a long-term solvency ratio that shows the relationship between total long-term debt and total shareholders' equity. It is calculated by dividing Total Debt by Total Equity.

concepts.title: Return on Capital Employed
concepts.description: Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) is a profitability ratio that measures the return earned by the business on the investment made by shareholders. It is calculated by dividing Net Profit by Capital Employed.

concepts.title: DuPont Method
concepts.description: The DuPont Method is a way to calculate Return on Equity (ROE) using the formula: ROE = Profit Margin x Asset Turnover x Equity Multiplier. Use the mnemonic 'Profit Margin' as 'Pigs Run' to remember the order of calculation.

concepts.title: Incorrect Calculation of Current Ratio
concepts.description: Students often make a mistake by calculating the Current Ratio by dividing Total Current Assets by Total Fixed Assets. Correct calculation involves dividing Total Current Assets by Total Current Liabilities.

concepts.title: Types of Ratios
concepts.description: There are four types of ratios: (1) Liquidity Ratios, (2) Solvency Ratios, (3) Profitability Ratios, and (4) Turnover Ratios. Remember the word 'LIST' to associate with these types.

concepts.title: Gross Profit Ratio
concepts.description: Gross Profit Ratio is a profitability ratio that measures the gross profit earned by the business as a percentage of its sales revenue. It is calculated by dividing Gross Profit by Sales Revenue.

concepts.title: Asset Turnover Ratio
concepts.description: Asset Turnover Ratio is a turnover ratio that measures the efficiency of a business in utilizing its assets to generate sales. It is calculated by dividing Sales Revenue by Total Assets.
